sections.heading: Atlas Configuration Options
sections.body:
You can change the following options of your Atlas cluster:

| Setting                                                 | Action                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Limitations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Cluster Type                                            | Change the cluster type.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | You can move from a Flex cluster to a dedicated cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Global Cluster Configuration                            | Enable Global Writes for your cluster or change existing global cluster configurations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | After you enable Global Writes for a cluster, you can't disable them.When Global Writes are enabled, Global Clusters do not support dedicated search nodes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Cloud Provider & Region                                 | Select a different provider to change the cloud provider for your dedicated clusters.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | If you add or move a node to a region without an existing primary or secondary node, Atlas requires each newly migrated replica set member to perform a Cloud-based initial sync.If you created an Atlas cluster on Google Cloud or Azure (Microsoft Azure) before November 2, 2020, when Atlas added support for multi-cloud clusters, changing to a different provider changes the connection string to your new cluster. Consider scheduling a time to update your applications with the new connection string to connect to the cluster again.To learn more, see Should I update the connection string if I change cloud provider on a dedicated cluster?For clusters on Azure (Microsoft Azure), if you change a region, some storage capabilities might be unavailable in that region. The Atlas UI notifies you that your IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) will be reduced, which might impact your application performance. To learn more, see regions that support extended storage, and Change Storage Capacity and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) on Azure (Microsoft Azure). |
| Deploy or Configure a multi-cloud, multi-region cluster | Deploy or modify a multi-cloud, multi-region cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | For clusters on Azure (Microsoft Azure), if you change to a multi-region cluster, some storage volume capabilities might be unavailable in one of the regions. The Atlas UI notifies you that your IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) will be reduced, which might impact your application performance. To learn more, see regions that support extended storage, and Change Storage Capacity and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) on Azure (Microsoft Azure).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Cluster Tier                                            | Change the cluster tier.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | If your cluster uses NVMe storage, Atlas must perform an initial sync.A File Copy Based Initial Sync will always be used to sync all of the nodes of an NVME cluster whenever an initial sync is required.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Cluster Storage Options                                 | Change the storage options for the cluster tier.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Clusters using NVMe (non-volatile memory express) storage have a fixed size for each cluster tier.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Cluster Autoscaling Options                             | Change the cluster's auto-scaling options, including storage auto-scaling. Depending on whether you choose to use storage auto-scaling, Atlas manages the oplog entries based on either the minimum oplog retention window, or the oplog size. To learn more, see Oplog Size Behavior. Atlas enables storage auto-scaling by default.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| MongoDB Version                                         | Upgrade the major MongoDB version of the cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | You can't downgrade the MongoDB version unless you pinned the cluster's FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading.You can't upgrade to a version that is 2 versions above the pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) version. If a cluster is pinned to FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) 6.0, you can only upgrade to 7.0, not 8.0+.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Deploy a Sharded Cluster                                | Scale up a replica set to a sharded cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | You can't reverse this operation.Atlas allows Sharded Clusters for M30 or larger clusters.You can convert a replica set to a sharded cluster when a database trigger is running with the Document Preimage configuration option enabled if your cluster runs MongoDB 5.3 or later. For clusters running MongoDB versions earlier than 5.3, you must disable the Document Preimage configuration option before converting to a sharded cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Modify the Number of Shards                             | Set the number of shards for a sharded cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Reducing the number of shards takes some time. Atlas removes shards in descending order based on the number in in their "\_id" field. Any subsequent MongoDB configuration changes start after Atlas removes the shards.IMPORTANT: When you remove a shard in 8.0, Atlas uses the moveCollection command to move any unsharded collections in that shard to a remaining shard. All unsharded collections remain online during this process.All sharded collections remain online and available during the shard removal process. You must turn on the balancer to drain the sharded collections from the removed shard.Atlas moves any unsharded collections that can't be drained by moveCollection command by using the movePrimary command. To learn more about the limitations of moveCollection, see Restrictions. movePrimary is an offline operation.For more information about shard removal, see Remove Shards from a Sharded Cluster.Atlas allows Sharded Clusters for M30 or larger clusters.                                                                                                     |
| Enable or Disable Backup                                | Enable or disable backups for the cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Atlas enables backups for Flex clusters automatically. You can't disable backup for clusters on those tiers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Enable or Disable the Business Intelligence Connector   | Enable or disable the BI Connector for Atlas for the cluster.The MongoDB Connector for Business Intelligence for Atlas (BI Connector) is only available for M10 and larger clusters.The BI Connector is a powerful tool which provides users SQL-based access to their MongoDB databases. As a result, the BI Connector performs operations which may be CPU and memory intensive. Given the limited hardware resources on M10 and M20 cluster tiers, you may experience performance degradation of the cluster when enabling the BI Connector. If this occurs, scale up to an M30 or larger cluster or disable the BI Connector. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| Manage your own encryption keys                         | Enable or disable using your own encryption keys with the cluster.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

Click [Apply Changes](scale-cluster-payment) after you complete any
of these modifications.

sections.heading: Migration, Availability, and Performance Impact
sections.body:
Making changes to a cluster often requires migrating to new servers
and storage volumes. The time required for an [initial sync](manual/reference/glossary) and
resynchronizing data across storage volumes increases linearly with the
amount of data in the cluster.

[A File Copy Based Initial Sync](manual/core/replica-set-sync) will always be used to
sync all of the nodes of an NVME cluster whenever an initial sync is required.

The following migrations require an [initial sync](manual/reference/glossary):

- Scaling up from a Free cluster (formerly known as `M0`) or a Flex cluster to a higher cluster tier.

- Changes from general to [NVMe storage volumes](nvme-storage) and
  from NVMe to general storage.

- Scaling up or scaling down from one NVMe (non-volatile memory express) cluster tier to another,
  initiated either manually or via auto-scaling. NVMe (non-volatile memory express) clusters
  auto-scale to the next higher tier when 90% of the available storage
  space is consumed.

- Changes that require a replacement of an NVMe-backed Atlas
  cluster, such as region changes.

- For clusters deployed to Azure (Microsoft Azure), changes to the [class](storage-class-ui).

To maximize availability:

- For a replica set, Atlas migrates one node at a time, starting
  with the secondary nodes first and then the primary.

- For a sharded cluster, Atlas performs the migration of the shards
  independently of each other. For each shard, such as a replica set,
  Atlas migrates one node at a time, starting with the secondary
  nodes first and then the primary.

[Retryable writes](manual/core/retryable-writes) should prevent any
write errors during the election of a new primary. On average, an
election can take five seconds.

Migration can affect performance if your primary is already reaching
operational capacity. Each newly migrated replica set node must
perform an [initial sync](manual/reference/glossary) from the primary, adding to the
operational load. Migrations can also affect performance if you set
[read preferences](manual/core/read-preference) to read from
secondaries. The replica set is down one secondary during the
migration.

If the workload on the Atlas cluster is such that it impedes
operations, including the ability to scale, MongoDB Atlas might, in
some situations, create indexes in your cluster as a safeguard.

sections.heading: Billing
sections.body:
As you change your cluster, you can compare the costs of different
options before applying them. The **Cluster Overview** box
displays the cost of the selected configuration, excluding data
transfer.

> **Important:** 
>
> Free Clusters
>
> Upgrading a Free cluster to a paid
> tier cluster starts billing for the cluster. See Manage Billing for
> complete documentation on Atlas billing.

The following sections provide complete documentation for each of the
Atlas cluster scaling configuration options.

sections.heading: Backup
sections.body:
See [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider) for more information on
backing up your cluster.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To modify a cluster, you must have [Project Cluster Manager](<Project Cluster Manager>)
access or higher to the project.

sections.heading: Cloud-based Initial Sync
sections.body:
When you add or replace a node in an Atlas cluster, Atlas
performs a Cloud-based initial sync, which leverages your cloud provider's native
capabilities to create a snapshot of the source node's data and restore
it to the new node. Atlas prioritizes restoring from a primary
node over secondary nodes to maximize data freshness.

If your Atlas cluster has nodes in multiple regions, including at
least one AWS (Amazon Web Services) region, you can [Enable Faster Cross-Region Initial Sync for AWS Clusters](enable_time_based_initial_sync_aws) in your
cluster's **Additional Settings** for an additional cost. This
allows Atlas to use [AWS time-based snapshot copies](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ebs/latest/userguide/time-based-copies.html)
to speed up cross-region data copy between AWS (Amazon Web Services) nodes during Cloud-based initial sync.
To learn more about this setting, see
[Enable Faster Cross-Region Initial Sync for AWS Clusters](enable_time_based_initial_sync_aws).

Cross-region Cloud-based initial sync is available for AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters only. For
Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud clusters, Cloud-based initial sync is only available when adding or
replacing nodes in the same region as the source node. You can add or
replace nodes in your cluster when you [edit the cluster configuration](edit-cluster-atlas).

> **Note:** 
>
> Self-Managed Initial Sync Disambiguation
>
> For self-managed deployments, initial sync is the process that
> MongoDB uses to add a new node to a replica set. Starting in MongoDB
> 5.2, the initial sync process can be *logical* or *file copy based*.
> These processes are different from Cloud-based initial syncs available in MongoDB
> Atlas, which leverage your cloud provider's native capabilities
> to create a snapshot of the source node's data and restore it to the
> new node. To learn more about the self-managed initial sync process,
> see [Initial Syncs](manual/core/replica-set-sync) in
> the MongoDB manual.

sections.heading: Edit a Cluster
sections.body:
**Atlascli:**

Atlas CLI

You can modify any of the cluster settings on this page using the Atlas CLI.

To update an Atlas cluster using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas clusters update [clusterName] [options]

```

To upgrade the cluster tier, disk size, and/or MongoDB version for an `M0` or Flex Atlas cluster using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas clusters upgrade [clusterName] [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas clusters update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-update) and
[atlas clusters upgrade](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-upgrade).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

sections.heading: Edit Advanced Settings
sections.body:
To update the advanced configuration settings for one cluster using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas clusters advancedSettings update <clusterName> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas clusters advancedSettings update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-advancedSettings-update).

**Ui:**

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: Edit the configuration
sections.body:
For the cluster you want to modify, click the
ellipsis-h icon, and then select **Edit Configuration**.

Alternatively, if you are already viewing the specific
cluster, click the **Configuration** button. For
Free clusters, you can also click the
**Upgrade** button for the cluster.

sections.heading: Modify the Cluster Type
sections.body:
If you have a Free cluster or Flex cluster, you can change it to a dedicated
cluster.

To scale up your Free cluster or Flex cluster to an `M10+` cluster, complete the following steps:

sections.heading: Step 1: Click  and select your cluster from the drop-down menu.
Step 2: At the top of your configuration settings, click the  tab.
Step 3: Select your preferred cluster tier.
sections.body:
To learn more, see [Modify the Cluster Tier](scale-cluster-instance).

> **Note:** 
>
> Considerations
>
> You can't change a dedicated cluster to a Free cluster or a Flex cluster.

sections.heading: Modify the Global Cluster Configuration
sections.body:
> **Important:** You can't disable Global Writes for a cluster once deployed.

You can enable global writes for your cluster or modify
existing global cluster configurations.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Manage Global Clusters](global-clusters).
>
> - [Create a Global Cluster](create-new-global-write-cluster).

sections.heading: Modify the Cloud Provider & Region
sections.body:
> **Note:** 
>
> Considerations
>
> Free cluster Tier Clusters
>
> You can modify the cloud provider and region only when
> you scale up your Free cluster to a larger cluster.
>
> Flex cluster or larger Tier Clusters
>
> You can modify the cloud provider and region when you
> modify your cluster or scale up to a larger cluster.
>
> Changing to a different provider could change the connection string to
> your new cluster if your old cluster was deployed on Google Cloud or
> Azure (Microsoft Azure) before October 2020. Consider scheduling a time to [update your applications with the new connection string](atlas-faq-migrate-providers) to resume connectivity to the cluster.
> Atlas migrates data to the new cluster.

- To view the current cloud providers and regions for your cluster,
  select **Cloud Provider & Region**.

- To modify the cloud providers and regions applied to your cluster,
  follow the procedures on [highest priority region](deploy-across-multiple-regions).

- To add electable nodes to your cluster during a regional outage,
  follow the procedure on [Reconfigure a Replica Set During a Regional Outage](reconfigure-rs-during-regional-outage).

- To scale up from an Atlas Free cluster or Flex cluster,
  select from the available cloud providers.

- If your cluster contains [Search Nodes](configure-search-nodes),
  you can only move it to regions that support Search Nodes. For a
  full list of regions that don't support Search Nodes, see
  [Cloud Provider Regions](fts-cloud-provider-regions).

- If you change the primary region for a cluster with Cloud Backup
  enabled, Atlas takes an on-demand snapshot and stores it in the
  new primary region without waiting for the next scheduled snapshot. If
  the new primary region was previously a secondary backup region for
  [snapshot copy distribution](snapshot-distribution), Atlas
  automatically updates your [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy) to distribute snapshot copies from the new
  primary region to the original primary region and other secondary
  regions.

- To remove or deploy additional Search Nodes, adjust the
  **Number of Search Nodes** setting under **Search
  nodes for workload isolation**. You can deploy between 2 and 32 search
  nodes on your cluster.

  If you delete all existing Search Nodes on your cluster, there will
  be an interruption in processing your search query results while
  Atlas migrates from `mongot` processes running separately on
  dedicated Search Nodes to `mongot` processes running alongside
  `mongod`. You can't run queries while indexes are building on the
  node that hosts both the `mongot` and `mongod` processes, and you
  will experience query downtime.

  When you add Search Nodes to a cluster that doesn't have any search
  nodes, the existing `mongot` processes running alongside `mongod`
  tail writes to the database that happen after MongoDB Search completed the
  initial sync and the `mongot` processes on the new dedicated search
  nodes perform an initial sync on the required collections. This
  results in dual reads during the migration process.

  To learn more, see [Add Dedicated Search Nodes](fts-add-search-nodes-to-prod).

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Cloud Providers and Regions](create-cluster-cloud-provider-region).
>
> - [Move a Cluster to a Different Region](move-cluster).

sections.heading: View Available Regions
sections.body:
To list available regions that Atlas supports for new deployments using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas clusters availableRegions list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas clusters availableRegions list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-availableRegions-list).

sections.heading: Modify the Cluster Tier
sections.body:
You can change the cluster tier, as well as the memory, storage,
and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) (speed) specifications for the selected cluster. If you
deployed Search Nodes separately for an `M10` or higher cluster,
you can also change your search tier.

> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't modify the cluster tier to a tier that doesn't support
> cloud backup.

sections.heading: Free Cluster and Flex Cluster Considerations
sections.body:
- You can't scale down an `M10+` dedicated cluster to a
  Free cluster or a Flex cluster.

- Changing the cluster tier requires downtime in the following
  scenarios:

- You change from a Free cluster or a Flex cluster to
  an `M10` or larger cluster tier.

- You change from a Free cluster to a Flex cluster.

  To prevent data corruption, halt write operations to your cluster
  for the duration of your scale operation.

sections.heading: Tier for Cluster Nodes
sections.body:
Select your preferred cluster tier. If you deployed Search Nodes
separately, in the **Base Tier** tab, select your preferred
cluster tier. The selected instance size dictates
the memory, storage, vCPUs, and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) specification for each
data-bearing server [^data-bearing] in the cluster.

> **Warning:** Upgrading from a free or Flex cluster to a dedicated cluster
> tier deletes the current cluster. To keep your existing snapshots,
> [download those snapshots](restore-overview) before upgrading
> your cluster.

> **Note:** Atlas reboots all nodes in your cluster when you modify the cluster tier.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [base snapshot schedule](cloud-provider-retention-policy)
>
> - [Select Cluster Tier](create-cluster-instance)

From the **Cluster Tier** section, you can also:

- [Customize Cluster Storage](create-cluster-storage)

- [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling)

You can [Select Cluster Tier](create-cluster-instance) appropriately
sized for your analytics workload. To learn more, see [Analytics Nodes for Workload Isolation](deploy-analytics-nodes).

> **Important:** After upgrading a free to a dedicated cluster, a free to a
> Flex cluster, or a Flex cluster to a dedicated cluster, you
> must restart your applications before connecting to the upgraded
> cluster.

If you deploy [read-only nodes](manual/reference/glossary) and want your
cluster to scale faster, consider adjusting your [Replica Set Scaling Mode](replica-set-scaling-mode).

sections.heading: Tier for Search Nodes
sections.body:
In the **Search Tier** tab, select your preferred search tier.
If the search tier that you select is not available in your region,
Atlas automatically switches to the next higher search tier in your
region. The selected instance size dictates the memory, storage, IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
specification, and cost of each search node on your cluster.

> **Note:** Atlas reboots all nodes in your cluster when you modify the search tier.

sections.heading: Modify Additional Settings
sections.body:
You can set the following options:

- [modifying the cluster](scale-cluster-version)

- [Enable or Disable Backup for the Cluster](scale-cluster-backups)

- [Enable or Disable Termination Protection for the Cluster](scale-cluster-termination-protection)

- [Scale your Replica Set to a Sharded Cluster](scale-cluster-sharding)

- [increase the number of shards](scale-cluster-shardNum)

- [Enable or Disable BI Connector for Atlas for the Cluster](scale-cluster-enable-bi)

- [Encryption at Rest](scale-cluster-enable-encryption)

- [Configure Additional Configuration Options](scale-cluster-more-configuration-options)

sections.heading: Upgrade the MongoDB Version of the Cluster
sections.body:
> **Important:** Before you upgrade your cluster, refer to the current
> recommended best practices for major version upgrades.

- Select **Additional Settings** to view the currently
  configured MongoDB version for the cluster.

  Atlas always upgrades the cluster to the latest patch release of
  the specified major version. The upgrade uses a rolling update
  process to maintain cluster availability.

  You can't downgrade the cluster to an earlier MongoDB version unless
  you pinned the cluster's FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading.

  You can switch from using the Latest Release to using a
  specific release only if the latest MongoDB version is a major
  release.

- From the **Select a version** dropdown, select the new
  MongoDB version.

  Atlas supports the following upgrade paths:

  - MongoDB 6.0 -> MongoDB 7.0

  - MongoDB 7.0 -> MongoDB 8.0

  - MongoDB 8.0 -> Latest Release

sections.heading: Enable or Disable Backup for the Cluster
sections.body:
Atlas automatically enables backups for Flex clusters and you
can't disable this setting. To learn more, see [Flex Cluster Backups](flex-snapshots).

To enable Cloud Backup for an `M10+` Atlas cluster, set
the Turn on Cloud Backup toggle to On. When
enabled, Atlas takes snapshots of your databases at regular
intervals and retains them according to your project's [base snapshot schedule](cloud-provider-retention-policy).

To disable Cloud Backup for an `M10+` Atlas cluster, set the
Turn on Cloud Backup toggle to Off. This
also disables Continuous Cloud Backup for the cluster. To retain any existing
snapshots after Cloud Backup is disabled, set the Keep
existing snapshots after backups disabled? toggle to On
before you apply your changes to the cluster. This option instructs
Atlas to retain the existing snapshots and [oplog](manual/reference/glossary) for the
cluster according to your cluster's [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy).

For detailed descriptions of the available backup options, see
[Configure Backup Options for the Cluster](create-cluster-backups).

sections.heading: Enable or Disable Termination Protection for the Cluster
sections.body:
To enable **Termination Protection** for a cluster, toggle
**Termination Protection** to **Yes**.

If enabled, Atlas prevents users from deleting the
cluster. To delete a cluster that has termination protection
enabled, you must first disable termination protection. By default,
Atlas disables termination protection for
all clusters.

> **See Also:** [Terminate active clusters](terminate-cluster).

sections.heading: Scale your Replica Set to a Sharded Cluster
sections.body:
You can convert a replica set to a sharded cluster when
a [database trigger](atlas-database-trigger) is running
with the Document Preimage configuration option enabled
if your cluster runs MongoDB 5.3 or later. For clusters running
MongoDB versions earlier than 5.3, you must disable the
Document Preimage configuration option before converting
to a sharded cluster.

To deploy your cluster as a [sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary),
toggle **Shard your cluster (M30 and up)** to `Yes`.

To scale up a replica set to a multi-sharded cluster,
you must scale up to a **single** shard cluster first,
restart your application and **reconnect to the cluster**, and then add
additional shards.

If you don't restart the application clients, your data might be inconsistent
once Atlas begins distributing data across shards.

If you don't reconnect the application clients, your application may suffer
from data outages.

- If you are using a
  [DNS Seed List](manual/reference/connection-string)
  connection string, your application automatically connects to the
  [mongos](manual/reference/program/mongos) for your sharded cluster.

- If you are using a
  [standard connection string](manual/reference/connection-string),
  you must update your connection string to reflect your new cluster
  topology.

> **See Also:** [Deploy a Sharded Cluster](create-cluster-sharding).

sections.heading: Modify the Number of Shards
sections.body:
This field is visible only if the deployment is a sharded cluster.

Your cluster can have between 1 and 70 shards, inclusive.

To scale up a replica set to a multi-sharded cluster,
you must scale up to a **single** shard cluster first,
restart your application and **reconnect to the cluster**, and then add
additional shards.

If you don't reconnect the application clients, your application may suffer
from data outages.

After you scale up a replica set cluster to a single-shard cluster,
you can set the number of shards to deploy with the sharded cluster.

If you are reducing the number of shards in your sharded cluster,
Atlas removes shards in descending order based on the number in the
`"_id"` field (see [Sharded Cluster Configuration](manual/tutorial/view-sharded-cluster-configuration)).
For example, consider a sharded cluster with the following three shards:

- `"shard0"`

- `"shard1"`

- `"shard2"`

If you set the number of shards to two, Atlas
removes `"shard2"` from the cluster.

IMPORTANT: When you remove a shard in 8.0, Atlas uses the
[moveCollection](manual/reference/command/moveCollection) command to move any unsharded collections in
that shard to a remaining shard. All unsharded collections remain online
during this process.

- All sharded collections remain online and available during the shard removal
  process. You must turn on the balancer to drain the sharded collections from
  the removed shard.

- Atlas moves any unsharded collections that can't be drained by `moveCollection` command
  by using the [movePrimary](manual/reference/command/movePrimary) command. To learn more about
  the limitations of `moveCollection`, see [Restrictions](manual/reference/command/moveCollection).
  `movePrimary` is an offline operation.

- For more information about shard removal, see
  [Remove Shards from a Sharded Cluster](manual/tutorial/remove-shards-from-cluster).

Don't create a sharded cluster with a single shard for production
environments. Single-shard sharded clusters don't provide the same
benefits as multi-shard configurations. After you create a single-shard
cluster, restart your application, reconnect to the cluster,
and then add more shards to your cluster.

sections.heading: Enable or Disable BI Connector for Atlas for the Cluster
sections.body:
To enable [BI Connector for Atlas](bi-connector) for this cluster, toggle
**Enable Business Intelligence Connector (M10 and up)** to
**Yes**.

> **See Also:** [Enable BI Connector for Atlas](create-cluster-enable-bi).

sections.heading: Enable Encryption at Rest
sections.body:
To enable Atlas Encryption at Rest for this cluster using your
KMS (Key Management Service), toggle **Manage your own encryption keys (M10 and up)**
to **Yes**. To learn more, see
[Manage Your Own Encryption Keys](create-cluster-enable-encryption).

> **Note:** All changes to [customer KMS](manual/security-kms-encryption)
> require an [initial sync](manual/core/replica-set-sync).

sections.heading: Configure Additional Configuration Options
sections.body:
Configure additional options for your cluster from this section.

For details on these options, see
[Configure Additional Options](create-cluster-more-configuration-options).

sections.heading: Modify Cluster Details
sections.body:
Add tags, view existing tags, and manage tags. To learn more, see
[Manage cluster Tags When You Modify an Existing Cluster](manage-tags-modify-cluster).

> **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
> Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
> resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
> resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
> and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
> private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
> [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

sections.heading: Save Your Changes for Later
sections.body:
Click **Save Draft** at the end of your cluster configuration form
to save your changes.

Clicking **Save Draft** the first time prompts Atlas to send an email to the
email address associated with your Atlas account with a button to
return to your modified cluster configuration form.

You can return to the form to:

- Continue making changes by clicking **Continue** on the pop-up that appears.

- Reset your changes made by clicking **Reset** on
  the pop-up that appears.

- [Review and apply](review-apply-cluster-changes) your cluster changes by
  clicking **Continue** on the pop-up that appears, then **Review Changes**.

sections.heading: Review and Apply Your Changes
sections.body:
Click **Review Changes** to review the changes you have made.

The **Review Changes** page displays a complete side-by-side
summary of the modified attributes with any warnings or notes
pertaining to the changes. The original attribute settings are listed
on the left and the corresponding new settings with changes in pricing
are listed on the right.

Atlas displays all warnings and notes related to the change at the
top. These include changes that:

- Can't be rolled back.

- Require an [initial sync](manual/core/replica-set-sync).

- Result in expected delays in execution, increase in workload, or
  downtime.

Once you have reviewed the changes, click **Apply Changes** to
apply them to your cluster.

If you are upgrading from a Free cluster, Atlas
prompts you to enter payment information before applying your changes.

[^data-bearing]: For replica sets, the data-bearing servers are the servers hosting the
    replica set nodes. For sharded clusters, the data-bearing servers are the
    servers hosting the shards. For sharded clusters, Atlas also deploys
    servers for the [config servers](manual/core/sharded-cluster-config-servers); these are
    charged at a rate separate from the cluster costs.

sections.heading: Convert a Replica Set to a Sharded Cluster
sections.body:
You can convert a [replica set](manual/reference/glossary) to a [sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary).

> **Note:** Atlas reboots all nodes in your [replica set](manual/reference/glossary) when you
> convert it to a [sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary).

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To convert your replica set to a sharded cluster:

- Your cluster must be an `M30+` cluster.

- You must have the [Project Cluster Manager](<Project Cluster Manager>) or higher role.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
- For the cluster you want to modify, click ,
  then select Edit Configuration.

- Expand Additional Settings to modify your cluster
  configuration.

- Enable sharding in the Shard your cluster section
  by setting the toggle to On.

  IMPORTANT: After you convert a replica set to a sharded
  cluster, you can't convert it again to a replica set.

  Atlas automatically selects 1 Shard from the
  drop-down menu. You can increase the number of shards in a later
  step after you restart your application clients.

  WARNING

  Queries using [$unionWith](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/unionWith) with [$search](pipe.$search) in the
  sub-pipeline might fail during an upgrade from a replica set to a
  sharded cluster running MongoDB v7.0 if the collection is not spread
  across multiple shards. To mitigate this, deploy more than one shard
  on your cluster and shard the collection such that multiple chunks
  are spread across multiple shards.

- Click Review Changes to review the changes to
  billing and click Apply Changes.

It may take some time for Atlas to deploy the changes. Please
wait until Atlas has converted your cluster before proceeding to
the next step.

If you don't restart the application clients, your data might be
inconsistent after Atlas begins distributing data across shards.

- If you are using a [DNS Seed List](manual/reference/connection-string)
  connection string, your application automatically connects to the
  [mongos](manual/reference/program/mongos) for your sharded cluster after you restart your
  application.

- If you are using a [standard connection string](manual/reference/connection-string),
  you must update your connection string to reflect your new cluster
  topology.

- If you are using private endpoints to connect to your Atlas
  cluster, your cluster experiences downtime during the
  conversion.

To learn more, see Connect to an Atlas Cluster.

- For the cluster you want to modify, click ,
  then select Edit Configuration.

- Expand Additional Settings to modify your cluster
  configuration.

- Select the number of shards from the drop-down menu.

- Click Review Changes to review the changes to
  billing and click Apply Changes.

Sharding supports high throughput and large datasets, and you can
increase the number of shards as data requirements grow.

To shard the collection whose data you want to distribute, see
[sh.shardCollection()](manual/reference/method/sh.shardCollection) for more
information.

WARNING: If you shard a collection that already has a MongoDB Search
index, you might experience a brief period of incomplete search results
while the index is building on a shard. Also, if you add a shard for
an already sharded collection that contains a MongoDB Search index, your search
queries against that collection might return incomplete results until
the initial sync process completes on the added shards. To learn more,
see [initial sync process](troubleshoot-initial-sync).

If you want to make further changes to the [sharded cluster](manual/reference/glossary), see [modify all configuration options](scale-cluster) for more information
on the cluster-wide settings that you can modify.
